name: Mojo
summary: Modular's Mojo grafts Rust-style ownership onto Python syntax with one distinctive twist: destruction at last use (sub-expression granularity) rather than end of scope. Argument conventions are borrowed (default, immutable shared), inout (mutable unique), and owned/var (consuming); the transfer operator ^ ends the source binding's lifetime explicitly. Mojo's origins (lifetime parameters) are tracked symbolically by the compiler; per-field destruction tracks partial moves of struct fields independently. Ownership shipped with the first public release in 2023; lifetimes/origins stabilized 2024–2026.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Modular's **Mojo** grafts Rust-style ownership onto Python syntax with one distinctive twist: destruction at *last use* (sub-expression granularity) rather than end of scope.

Argument conventions are `borrowed` (default, immutable shared), `inout` (mutable unique), and `owned`/`var` (consuming); the transfer operator `^` ends the source binding's lifetime explicitly.

sections.title: ASAP destruction — last use, sub-expression granularity
sections.content:
The lifetime checker calls `__del__` immediately after a value's last use within an expression — intermediates in `a + b + c + d` die between sub-ops — in contrast to Rust's end-of-scope drops.

This sub-expression granularity is the distinctive twist: it minimises live ranges, allows aggressive register reuse, and makes the lifetime model match the structure of the dataflow rather than the structure of the syntactic scope.

sources.label: Modular — Deep dive into ownership in Mojo

sections.title: Origins and per-field partial moves
sections.content: Mojo's "origins" (lifetime parameters) are tracked symbolically by the compiler; per-field destruction tracks partial moves of struct fields independently — a field-granular generalisation of Rust's whole-binding move tracking.

sections.title: Status
sections.content: Ownership shipped with the first public release in 2023; lifetimes/origins stabilized 2024–2026. The compile-time parameter system is covered separately as `mojo-parameter`; the codegen pipeline is `mojo-kgen`.

sources.label: Modular — Deep dive into ownership in Mojo
